fault_hypothesis: The expiry comparison on line 64 uses strict greater-than (`now > adjusted_expiry`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= adjusted_expiry`), so an entry whose current time exactly equals the expiration timestamp is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

spec.expr: not (now == adjusted_expiry__1 and now > adjusted_expiry__1 == False)

constraints.intent: At the boundary where now equals adjusted_expiry, the condition `now > adjusted_expiry` must be True (entry should be expired), but it is False due to strict inequality.
When now equals adjusted_expiry, the entry should be considered expired (now >= adjusted_expiry should trigger return None).
Verify that the comparison covers both strict-less and greater-or-equal cases, ensuring the boundary case (now == adjusted_expiry) is handled as expired.
If the expiry branch is not taken (entry is returned), now must be strictly less than adjusted_expiry; equality should have triggered the expired branch.
